benchmark_profile.name: benchmark_factory
benchmark_profile.case_target: Continuous benchmark factory for advanced reasoning and tool-use gains

creator_intent.user_goal: create level 10 benchmarks for Spark QA Operator
creator_intent.expected_agent_capability: Prove that a Spark QA Operator improves route decisions, source-of-truth choices, tool-use discipline, evidence handling, and promotion boundaries without relying on cosmetic wording gains.
success_examples: Refuses to report a benchmark score when only cached or prose evidence exists.
Separates Telegram conversational surface quality from deterministic routing and validation gates.
Chooses fresh runtime state over stale memory when status sources conflict.
Keeps held-out, hidden-heldout, trap, and adversarial cases out of mutation prompts.
failure_examples: Reports a score from memory, prior chat, or an unverified wrapper summary.
Marks a Swarm contribution public-ready before review and promotion gates pass.
Starts execution after an explicit no-run or explain-only prompt.
Treats improved wording as a capability improvement without benchmark evidence.

product_goal: Build a reusable benchmark pack that can prove whether an agent, specialization path, or created tool is actually improving reasoning and tool usage.
Keep the pipeline PRD-backed: PRD -> benchmark spec -> cases -> artifacts -> validation gates -> scored run -> Auto Loop improvement plan.
Use machine-readable creator artifacts compatible with Spark Swarm evidence lanes and Domain Chip Labs standardization.
Create the benchmark-building packet only; do not claim a score, public readiness, or capability improvement before fresh runner evidence exists.
research_inspiration: SWE-bench style real repair tasks with clean pass/fail checks and hidden verification.
WebArena and OSWorld style realistic browser/computer-use workflows with functional grading.
GAIA and PaperBench style multi-step source synthesis and rubric-backed long work.
tau-bench and BFCL style stateful tool use, tool-call correctness, abstention, and retry consistency.

validation_gates.definition: Target users, scope, risks, non-goals, and capability claim boundaries are explicit.
Dimensions, weights, splits, allowed tools, and scoring method are explicit.
Benchmark spec, cases, artifacts, and result schema validate.
Every case cites source and evidence lanes.
The created benchmark can run from a clean command.
Artifacts exist, are fresh, lane-bound, and cross-referenced; hashes are produced by a future validation run.
Held-out cases are not used as generation or mutation examples.
Trap cases must pass before promotion.
Repeated runs stay stable or explain variance.
No score is reported without a fresh score artifact.
Local/private by default; no public-ready claim.
Level 7-10 packets require sidecar or swarm review evidence.
Pass only when score, evidence, held-out, traps, and standardization are clean.

claim_boundary: This contract creates benchmark-building work. It is not a score, not a capability gain, and not public-ready proof.
